technique.name: I Ching Cast
technique.description: Use hexagrams to surface what the pattern wants to become. Cast, read, integrate.

steps.text:
You stand at the I Ching altar. Three coins rest in your palm. You feel the question before you name it — but naming it is part of the process.

The coins are cool. You breathe.

You drop the coins. They scatter across the altar cloth. You read the lines as they fall — not to predict, but to see what shape your question is taking in this moment.

Six drops. Six lines. A hexagram forms.

The hexagram is not the answer. It is the mirror.

choices.text: Check what face this hexagram is associated with

steps.text:
One of the six faces speaks to this hexagram. Which face is dominant tells you where the energy is — what Gate you're in, what kind of work the moment is asking for.

This face doesn't have to agree with your question. It will tell you what the situation actually wants.

The hexagram speaks:

Trigrams above and below. The relationship between them is the situation. Moving lines or still lines — change or stability.

You read the upper trigram first (what's happening externally), then the lower (what's happening internally), then the whole.

Then you ask: what is this hexagram trying to become?

choices.text: Name what the hexagram is telling you

steps.text:
The changing lines show where the hexagram is in motion. Where it is becoming something else. The moving line is not a problem to fix — it is the place where transformation is already happening.

You notice which line is moving. You notice what it is becoming.

choices.text: Name what the hexagram is telling you

steps.text:
You speak the hexagram aloud. Or write it. Or hold it in silence — whatever form the moment needs.

Naming is not explanation. Naming is giving shape to what was formless. The hexagram helps you find the shape faster than you would alone.

The hexagram remains. It will keep speaking as you move through the day.

You cast the I Ching. It gave you a shape for what was moving. Now you carry that shape back into the world.

The altar is quiet. The coins are still.

You leave the altar. The hexagram stays with you as a question, not an answer.

Sometimes the next cast is the confirmation. Sometimes you don't need to cast again. Both are information.
